title: Non-overlapping substring count
prompt: Read a pattern P followed by a text T, each on its own line of stdin. Count the number of non-overlapping occurrences of P inside T using a greedy left-to-right scan: once a match starts at index i, the next candidate match starts at index i + len(P). P is between 1 and 100 printable-ASCII characters; T is between 0 and 1000 printable-ASCII characters. Both lines are terminated by \n. Write the decimal count followed by a newline. Exit 0.
notes_input_convention: Zero reads P from argv[1] and T from argv[2] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability. All other languages read two lines from stdin (terminators stripped). The harness adapts per language.
